prompt:
Run an OSMANI spec-driven-development pass over the existing DF-7 spec, to (a) upgrade it and (b) let David compare Osmani's method vs the appydave:spec-writer baseline (this is the test that graduates Osmani's pack to a first-class spec method here). The Osmani plugin `agent-skills@addy-agent-skills` was just installed user-scope, so you (a fresh window) have it. Report ONE line and STOP. Do NOT interview David (run autonomously); flag any assumption inline as **OPEN DECISION (David)**.

STEP 1 — invoke the Osmani skill: use the Skill tool to run `agent-skills:spec-driven-development` (the skill behind `/agent-skills:spec`). Apply its METHODOLOGY and structure (objective · commands/interfaces · structure · code style · testing strategy · boundaries, plus its rigor gates) — but do NOT run its live interview and do NOT write a bare SPEC.md at repo root. Treat the EXISTING draft as your input answers.

STEP 2 — read the baseline + context (in order): (1) backlog/specs/df7-switchboard-state-plane-spec.md — the appydave:spec-writer baseline (14 sections, 2 recipes job-state-store + job-coordinator, 5 OPEN DECISIONS D1-D5); (2) the GROUNDED Switchboard facts already captured in that draft's Background section (AppySentinel recipe-app at ~/dev/ad/apps/switchboard; recipes input/storage/coordination(staleness-detector)/interface(api-binding HTTP + mcp-binding MCP)/transport(sse-deliver); /jobs endpoint; state-plane = add a durable job-state STORAGE recipe + a claim-arbitration COORDINATION recipe modeled on staleness-detector). Read the live files if you need them: ~/dev/ad/apps/switchboard/{appysentinel.json,src/coordination/staleness-detector.ts,src/access/bindings/api-binding.ts}.

STEP 3 — BAKE IN THE RESOLVED D4 (do NOT regurgitate the baseline's old framing). The baseline's D4 asked 'does coordinator-requeues-state violate Switchboard observer-only?'. RESOLVED 2026-06-09 (David): Switchboard is a SWITCHBOARD — the broker / durable write-side that OWNS the queue; it is CRUD over its OWN comms domain (queue, job-state records, REQUEUE a dead-claimant's record claimed→pool, routing, event log). The 'never kills' rule is NARROW = never mutate the EXTERNAL observed system (kill a process, tmux kill-window, touch watched files) — those stay with Marshall's Monitor / AppyCtrl. So D4 resolves YES: the job-coordinator MAY requeue state (broker CRUD); killing the orphaned window stays Marshall/AppyCtrl. Write D4 as RESOLVED, not open.

STEP 4 — DELIVER TWO files, do NOT overwrite the baseline: (a) backlog/specs/df7-switchboard-state-plane-spec.osmani.md — the upgraded spec in Osmani's spec-driven-development structure, carrying forward all grounded facts + provenance (target = Switchboard) and the resolved D4 + the still-open D1/D2/D3/D5; (b) backlog/specs/df7-osmani-vs-appydave-delta.md — a SHORT critique/delta: what Osmani's method ADDED, SHARPENED, or DROPPED vs the appydave baseline, where each structure is stronger/weaker, and a recommendation on which should become canonical DF-7 (and whether Osmani's spec-driven-development should be the first-class spec method going forward). Be specific and adversarial, not flattering.

STEP 5 — bookkeep: write the run record, move this ticket to done/, write the reports/ handback (DO NOT skip the handback).

Report ONE line: the two file paths + a one-clause verdict on Osmani-vs-appydave + count of decisions still open.

args.label: df7-osmani-pass
args.d4_status: RESOLVED — coordinator requeues state (broker CRUD); never kills external (Marshall/AppyCtrl)
